<!-- Chart CH-TIM-14 INDEXED LINE, role-based (themeable) -->

## INDEXED LINE chart (CH-TIM-14)

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Colors are ROLES, not hex: resolve each role against the active design
language token block (a DL-xx block pasted next to this one). A role
chain like `benchmark -> muted` means use the first role the palette
defines.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-TIM-14",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "trend",
    "comparison",
    "change-over-time"
  ],
  "form": "line, all series rebased to 100",
  "data_required": "ordered periods + 2-4 series in DIFFERENT units or magnitudes; rebased to first period = 100",
  "family": "TIM",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-TIM-01 (same-unit series)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "series share one unit and scale",
      "use": "CH-TIM-01"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- comparing growth across series with different units or magnitudes
- cost escalation, price indices, relative performance since a baseline

Do NOT use when:
- absolute levels matter (rebasing hides them; use CH-TIM-01)
- one series only (an index of one is just a line)

### Input data shape
```json
{
  "title": "action title stating the relative move",
  "eyebrow": "caps, DL chrome only",
  "source": "bottom-left always",
  "unit": "index points; pass empty unit by convention",
  "x": "ordered periods",
  "series": "RAW values; the renderer rebases each to index_base at period 1",
  "annotation": "optional"
}
```

### Exact layout (px on the canvas, follow literally)

```json
{
  "canvas": {
    "width": 960,
    "height": 540
  },
  "layout": {
    "title": {
      "x": 32,

    },

      "letter_spacing": 2
    },
    "source": {
      "x": 32,
      "y": 516,
      "size": 11
    },
    "plot": {
      "left": 96,
      "right": 756,
      "top": 128,
      "bottom": 452
    },

      "size": 11,
      "offset": 12
    },
    "x_label": {
      "size": 11,
      "offset": 20
    },
    "series_label": {
      "size": 13,
      "offset": 10
    },
    "stroke": {
      "key": 2.5,
      "context": 2
    },
    "annotation": {
      "size": 12,
      "style": "italic"
    }
  },
  "scale": {
    "index_base": 100,
    "target_ticks": 5,
    "nice_steps": [
      1,
      2,
      5,
      10
    ],
    "note": "y floor = largest tick multiple <= data min; non-zero baseline is DECLARED: an index measures relative change and 100 is the reference"
  }
}
```

### Rules
- renderer rebases raw values: v / first * 100 (rounded to 0.1)
- the 100 line renders in the index_base_line role, stronger than gridlines
- non-zero y floor is legal HERE ONLY; this entry records the justification
- series labeled at line ends

### Never do this
- indexing series that share a unit (hides real gaps)
- hiding the base period
- reading index points as percent without saying so (132 = +32%)

### QA before delivering
- [ ] all series equal 100 at period 1
- [ ] 100 reference line visible
- [ ] title translates the index into percent change
- [ ] no legend
- [ ] source present

### Color roles

| Element | Role chain |
|---|---|
| background | background |
| key | primary |
| context | benchmark -> muted |
| gridline | gridline |
| zero_line | hairline |
| tick_label | body |
| x_label | body |
| key_label | primary |
| context_label | body |
| title | ink |
| source | body |
| annotation | body |
| index_base_line | benchmark -> muted |

### Fonts

Title uses the token block's `display` font; everything else uses
`body`. Weight 600 only on highlighted items.
